takeaways: CBRE/Colliers forecast CRE investment activity rising 16% to $562B in 2026 with the 10-Year Treasury holding 4.00–4.25% — capital is re-entering senior housing financing.
JLL data shows CRE companies running AI pilots jumped from 5% to 92% in three years, and agentic AI is now automating procurement, pricing, and operations across portfolios.
Senior living discounts are set to vanish as 73% of operators expect assisted living occupancy gains, but staffing remains the #1 industry concern heading into Q2 2026.

items.headline: Senior living discounts to vanish as demand outpaces supply in 2026
items.summary: PERE's Look Ahead 2026 reports operators are pulling concessions as occupancy recovery accelerates and new construction starts remain depressed. 73% of operators expect assisted living occupancy gains and 72% project memory care growth — a tailwind for Lloyd Jones' AL/MC repositioning strategy.

items.headline: Align Real Estate files 415-unit Oakland senior housing tower plan
items.summary: Align Real Estate submitted plans on April 22, 2026 to replace a Rockridge Trader Joe's with 371 IL, 18 AL, and 26 MC units. The infill, transit-adjacent senior project signals continued investor appetite for high-barrier urban senior housing despite construction headwinds.

items.headline: Lument 2026 outlook: capital reopens, valuations set to rebound
items.summary: Lument's 2026 Senior Living Outlook flags occupancy recovery, valuation gains, and a return of construction starts, while staffing remains the top operating concern. Debt and equity capital are flowing again, particularly into AL and MC where rate growth is strongest.

items.headline: U.S. News names 1,614 'Best Senior Living' communities for 2026
items.summary: U.S. News & World Report's 2026 ratings, released April 14, recognized 1,614 communities for resident satisfaction and care quality, including 28 Meridian Senior Living properties. The list is increasingly used by adult children as a referral filter and is moving the needle on lead quality for top-rated operators.

items.headline: CBRE: U.S. CRE investment to climb 16% to $562B in 2026
items.summary: CBRE's 2026 Outlook projects total investment volume of roughly $562B — close to the 2015–2019 pre-pandemic average — driven by improving capital access, more lender participation, and steady-to-lower borrowing costs. Senior housing and multifamily are expected to lead the recovery.

items.headline: 10-Year Treasury holds 4.00–4.25% range, anchoring CRE pricing
items.summary: Most fixed-rate CRE loans are pricing off the 10-Year Treasury, which has stabilized at 4.00–4.25% since mid-2025. Conventional commercial mortgage rates now span roughly 5.02%–8.75%, giving sponsors a more predictable underwriting environment for refinances and acquisitions.

items.headline: Colliers forecasts 15–20% jump in CRE sales volume as cross-border capital returns
items.summary: Colliers expects 2026 transaction volume to rise 15–20% as institutional and cross-border investors re-enter the market. Senior housing operators with stabilized assets are positioned to benefit from compressed cap rates as buyer competition rebuilds.

items.headline: Deloitte: lower rates and stronger leasing fundamentals reset CRE in 2026
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 outlook flags that capital is flowing again, interest rates are easing, and leasing fundamentals are stabilizing or improving across most property types. Sentiment among CRE executives is the most constructive since 2022.

items.headline: JLL: CRE AI pilots leap from 5% to 92% of companies in three years
items.summary: A JLL report cited by ICSC shows CRE firms running AI pilots have surged from 5% to 92% in three years. Owners are now demanding measurable operational impact rather than feature checklists — a shift Lloyd Jones can leverage in vendor selection conversations.

items.headline: Agentic AI moves into procurement, pricing, and operations across real estate
items.summary: ICSC reports agentic AI is automating complex retail and CRE workflows — moving beyond content generation to autonomous decision-making for procurement, dynamic pricing, inventory, and event programming. Early adopters are seeing 10–15% efficiency gains in back-office functions.

items.headline: Inman: residential proptech 'reset' driven entirely by AI
items.summary: Inman's February 2026 analysis highlights that residential proptech is back in focus, with AI-native tools for lead qualification, tour scheduling, and resident communication leading the rebound. Senior housing operators can repurpose these tools for prospect family follow-up and CRM automation.

items.headline: AI proptech platform Dooritt launches with AR property tours and indoor mapping
items.summary: India-based Dooritt launched April 23, 2026, combining AI search with AR holographic tours, environmental scoring (noise, air quality), and an indoor-mapping layer dubbed 'AUG' — the 'Google Maps of indoor spaces.' Watch for similar features migrating into U.S. multifamily and senior housing leasing journeys.

items.headline: LinkedIn names Dan Shapero CEO; Mohak Shroff to lead Platforms & Digital Work
items.summary: LinkedIn announced April 22, 2026 that Dan Shapero will be CEO and Mohak Shroff will become President of Platforms & Digital Work, reshaping LinkedIn's AI and platform strategy inside Microsoft. Expect accelerated rollouts of AI hiring tools relevant to senior living's persistent staffing shortage.

items.headline: DDI: 71% of leaders report rising stress, 40% considering exit
items.summary: DDI's Global Leadership Forecast finds 71% of leaders are under increased stress and 40% are weighing leaving their roles, with burnout and 'quiet cracking' cited as primary drivers. Operators investing in middle-manager support and learning are seeing measurable retention lifts.

items.headline: BayCom Corp announces executive transition for next-phase growth
items.summary: BayCom Corp's board appointed three new executives effective April 13, 2026 to drive its next phase of growth, signaling continued community-bank consolidation pressure. Lloyd Jones should monitor regional lender leadership shifts that may affect existing relationship banking lines.

items.headline: 2026 leadership playbook: human + AI, flatter orgs, learning as retention
items.summary: Top 2026 leadership trends from DDI and SIY Global emphasize human + AI co-leadership, flatter structures, and treating learning as a retention strategy rather than a perk. Firms that 'learn fastest when strategies break' are projected to outperform peers in volatile sectors.

items.headline: Wellhub: 89% of employees perform better when prioritizing wellbeing
items.summary: Wellhub's State of Work-Life-Wellness 2026 report finds 89% of employees link wellbeing to higher performance, accelerating the shift from wellness-as-benefit to wellness-as-productivity-strategy. Senior housing operators can apply the same framework to caregiver retention programs.

items.headline: Personalized, biomarker-driven wellness goes mainstream in 2026
items.summary: Dr. Axe and Active Wellness identify hyper-personal, AI-driven wellness — biomarker nutrition, at-home diagnostics, and AI coaches — as the defining 2026 trend. Senior living wellness programs that integrate biomarker tracking can differentiate IL offerings to active older adults.

items.headline: Emotional fitness emerges as a distinct 2026 health category
items.summary: Who What Wear's 2026 wellness preview names 'emotional fitness' as a new pillar alongside physical fitness, focusing on cognitive performance and emotional resilience. Mood-altering scents, longevity scans, and supplements rounded out the trend list.

items.headline: Social cardio: HYROX events and run clubs reshape group fitness
items.summary: Active Wellness reports cardio in 2026 is becoming intentional and social — HYROX-style events, run clubs, and partner workouts are driving consistency where solo gym memberships have stalled. Senior living programming can borrow the social-accountability model for IL fitness offerings.
